ad70f960c2c4.title: Get your menu online — 77% of diners check before visiting
ad70f960c2c4.description: No online menu found. Most diners check the menu before committing. Without one, you lose the customer at the decision point.
ad70f960c2c4.management_implication: Every day without a menu is a percentage of potential customers lost.

7b5fed9aaedc.title: Close the hygiene gap — 4 to 5
7b5fed9aaedc.description: An FSA rating of 4 is visible on your Google listing and the FSA website. Customers see it before they see your menu. Address the inspector's specific findings.
7b5fed9aaedc.expected_upside: Remove the trust ceiling on your overall score

6cdb9974c9a4.title: Establish basic web presence — you don't exist online beyond Google
6cdb9974c9a4.description: No website, Facebook, or Instagram detected. Even a basic page improves discoverability and gives customers a reason to trust you before visiting.

256b93c70456.title: Fix the digital shopfront — you're losing walk-ins
256b93c70456.description: You deliver a strong experience but your online presence doesn't make it easy to act on. Can a new customer confirm your hours, see your menu, and book — all within 60 seconds on their phone? If not, that's your fix list.
256b93c70456.management_implication: Interest without conversion is vanity traffic.

cb990e407371.title: Guest experience outpaces formal trust record
cb990e407371.description: Guests enjoy the venue but the compliance record hasn't kept up. One poor inspection would make a private gap public. The fix is proactive compliance work, not more marketing.
cb990e407371.expected_upside: Close the gap before it becomes visible

cee68ccd21ae.title: Ranked #7 of 10 locally — peers are ahead
cee68ccd21ae.description: At percentile 33.3, customers in this area have higher-rated alternatives. This isn't a crisis but it means you're competing on convenience rather than reputation.

da0d94b63175.title: Compliance record weakest in peer set — creates inspection exposure
da0d94b63175.description: You trail peers by 1.4 points on trust. This isn't a label problem — it means customers are getting a measurably better experience on this dimension from your competitors.
